sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.76 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Does there exist an absolute constant$c$such that any finite$p$-group$P$has an abelian section$A$satisfying$|A|^c>|P|$? By a result of A.\,Yu.\,Olshanskii (Math. Notes, 23 (1978), 183--185) we cannot require$A$to be a subgroup. By a result of J.\,G.\,Thompson (J.~Algebra, 13 (1969), 149--151) the existence of such a section$A$would imply the existence of a class$2$subgroup$H$of$P$with$|H|^c >|P|$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
